audit_items.proof_trace: ReadLocker and WriteLocker dereference their RWLock* argument without null checks, but all call sites in the codebase (http_agent.cc lines 56, 94, 111, 127, 195) pass &_desc_lock — the address of a non-static member variable, which is always non-null in C++. RWLock itself contains only a pthread_rwlock_t value type with no pointer dereferences. No external attacker can influence the pointer passed to these constructors.
